name: Coedit-XL Supplemented Underrepresented High-Scoring NAEP Math Responses
statement: Morris and colleagues report that several NAEP math items had excess low scores and too few higher-score responses, so they used Coedit-XL to generate paraphrases of higher-scoring student responses as training data.
population_or_context: NAEP grade 4 and grade 8 math constructed-response items.
quantitative_note: The final approach achieved human-like agreement on nine of ten held-out items using QWK criteria.
evaluation_method: Quadratic weighted kappa against withheld human scores, plus demographic standardized mean difference bias checks.
sparsity_or_imbalance_motivation: High-scoring responses, especially score 2 and score 3 responses on some items, were underrepresented relative to low-scoring responses.
synthetic_data_research_role: Generated high-scoring paraphrases balanced training data before fine-tuning item-specific scorer models.
reported_metrics: QWK within 0.05 of human-human agreement on nine of ten items.
limitations: At least one item remained difficult because of imbalance and missing or variable contextual information.
source_locator: Abstract; Data Set Concerns; Data-Preprocessing: Dealing with Data Imbalances; Data Augmentation; Discussion.
